mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.6% in Jan 2014 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate exceeds 3-month moving average by 2% or more
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 427.8B in Jan 2014 shows sustained consumer activity
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 60-day returns in less than 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) near zero combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL growth slowing) signals extended monetary accommodation, reducing equity risk premiums and supporting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 234.6 in early 2014 reflect accommodative stance
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Joint condition triggers produce positive 20-day returns in less than 62% of instances, or the interaction effect (measured as coefficient on FEDFUNDS×CPIAUCSL term) is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing capacity and economic output, which supports corporate profitability and equity returns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.0 in Jan 2014 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 54% of instances when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals tight labor market conditions that compress corporate margins but boost consumer spending, with net positive effect on equity returns over 20 trading days when both signals align.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth > 150K jobs AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp in same period
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137.5M and UNRATE at 6.6% in Jan 2014 show concurrent labor strength
regime_hint: labor market tightening with sustained job creation
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces positive 20-day returns in less than 60% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 900K annualized rate signals strengthening residential investment and construction activity, which drives employment and consumer wealth effects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 900K annualized rate for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 999K in Dec 2013 reflects post-crisis housing recovery acceleration
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with housing market normalization
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 60-day returns in less than 57% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% while federal funds rate remains below 0.5% signals real rate compression that incentivizes equity allocation over bonds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates deeply negative in early 2014 with FEDFUNDS near zero and inflation stable
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: Joint condition produces positive 20-day returns in less than 61% of instances, or the real rate compression effect (measured as CPIAUCSL minus FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth (GDPC1 growth rate falling below 2% annualized) signals economic deceleration that triggers flight-to-quality equity flows and multiple expansion in defensive sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth concerns dominate.
candidate_trigger: Annualized GDPC1 growth rate falls below 2% in latest quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15965.6B in Q3 2013 reflects moderate post-crisis growth trajectory
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce negative 60-day returns in less than 52% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 (i.e., signal lacks predictive power for downside), over any 3-year rolling window.
